message:
test(sched): pin SCHED_IDLE/SCHED_DEADLINE bounds + negative-policy EINVAL (bd-gkml2, bd-gh8m4, bd-i5fto, bd-kkihe)

Four host-parity ABI regression tests:

bd-gkml2  sched_priority_bounds_sched_idle_are_zero
          Linux reports min=max=0 for SCHED_IDLE with clean errno.
bd-gh8m4  sched_priority_bounds_sched_deadline_are_zero
          SCHED_DEADLINE (policy 6) also bands at 0/0 even though it
          uses the deadline scheduler rather than a priority queue.
bd-i5fto  sched_get_priority_min_negative_policy_is_einval
          Complements the existing i32::MAX invalid-policy test by
          pinning the negative-policy branch.
bd-kkihe  sched_get_priority_max_negative_policy_is_einval
          Symmetric negative-policy pin for the max helper.

Verification (rch + CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc):
  cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdlib_abi_test sched_
                                                     44 passed
  cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-abi --tests -- -D warnings  clean
  cargo fmt --check                                 clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
